pi_items.source: GCompris-qt GitHub repository analysis + official documentation (https://gcompris.net/docbook/stable6/en/user-interface.html)

Comprehensive search of GCompris-qt repository (201 total activities listed on GitHub). No activity named 'cartesian_coordinates', 'Cartesian Coordinates', 'geogebra_coord', or similar exists in the official GCompris software.

GitHub repository: https://github.com/gcompris/GCompris-qt. Analyzed all 201 activity directories in src/activities/ - no cartesian_coordinates activity found. Geometry-related activities include: tangram, baby_tangram, frieze, redraw_symmetrical, graph-coloring, positions, leftright, path_encoding variants.

pi_items.verified_value: GCompris uses flat section names like 'math numeration', 'math arithmetic', 'math multiplication arithmetic', 'sciences experiment', 'discovery logic', 'puzzle', 'strategy'. No hierarchical multi-level category paths.
pi_items.source: ActivityInfo.qml file analysis for 20+ activities (https://github.com/gcompris/GCompris-qt/tree/master/src/activities). Official documentation: https://gcompris.net/docbook/stable6/en/user-interface.html states math has exactly THREE subcategories: numeration, arithmetic, and measures. No 'Geometry' subcategory exists.

pi_items.metadata_value: Implicitly claims Geometry is a subcategory of Mathematics
pi_items.verified_value: Math subcategories are: numeration, arithmetic, measures. Geometry is not a subcategory of Mathematics.
pi_items.source: Official GCompris documentation: https://gcompris.net/docbook/stable6/en/user-interface.html - 'For math activities, we have three subcategories: numeration, arithmetic and measures.'

pi_items.verified_value: 201 activities in GCompris-qt as of February 2025

privileged_info_summary: This task references the 'Cartesian Coordinates' activity in GCompris with category path ['Mathematics', 'Geometry']. However, this activity does not exist in the official GCompris-qt software (which contains 201 activities total). GCompris uses flat section-based categorization (e.g., 'math arithmetic', 'sciences experiment'), not hierarchical category paths. Official GCompris documentation confirms mathematics has exactly three subcategories (numeration, arithmetic, measures), with no 'Geometry' subcategory. This is a synthetically created task for benchmarking purposes, not based on actual GCompris software.
